questions.question: What is the distance in nautical miles between the following two coordinates? [[NEXT_LINE]]A = N44° 44' 46.98'' W93° 5' 43.10'' [[NEXT_LINE]]B = N44° 52' 57.09'' W93° 12' 42.54''

You need to replace the tie-down ropes for your parking "T" on the ramp. You need three separate ropes, one for each wing and one for the tail. The wing tie-downs are 1.68 m above the ground. The tail tie-down is 1.07 m above the asphalt. If you add 24 inches for tying knots, how much total rope do you need to buy?

You are flying at FL320 and trying to navigate around thunderstorms. Your weather radar is tilted -2.5° and the bottom of the radar beam is painting the ground (4,000' MSL) as displayed on the 40 nm ring on the MFD. Your radar has a 7-degree beam width. You want to place the center of the radar beam at the freezing level (FL120) at 40 nm to get the best picture of reflectivity. What tilt angle should you set the radar?

A news helicopter is hovering when it sees an airliner flying across its field of view from an 11 o'clock position to a 1 o'clock position in 61 seconds at the same altitude. If the aircraft are 4 SM apart, what is the airliner's airspeed?
How tall is a redwood tree if you are lying on the ground gazing at it from 1/4 SM away? The angle from your eyes to the top of the tree is 11.76°.

You are approaching your home airport on a 346 course. When you are 18 nm away, you hear your friend call up on the CTAF, and she reports her position as 13 nm and flying a 256 course. How far away from your friend are you?

As you are flying, you notice a large wildfire in front of you. After reporting the fire to ATC, you turn 60° to the right to get around the smoke. Once you are abeam the fire, you turn left to intercept your original course at a 60° angle. If you rejoin your course 9 NM past where you started deviating, how much distance did you travel during the deviation?

Fresh from a long range navigation lecture where you learned about finding a course using latitude and longitude coordinates, you wish to impress your CFI at your upcoming lesson. You tell him that the distance between a degree of latitude is 60 NM and the distance between a degree of longitude can be calculated since it varies from 60 NM at the equator to zero at the Earth's poles. So he says, "Ok, hot shot, using these points, one at N48°30' W101°22' and the other at N48°30' W99°47', what is the distance between them?"

Two aircraft fly over the same VOR at the same time at different altitudes. Aircraft Alpha flies outbound on the 283° radial and aircraft Bravo flies outbound on the 037° radial. At the time aircraft Alpha is reading 40 DME, aircraft Bravo is reading 35 DME. At this point, how far apart are the aircraft from each other? Disregard slant range error.
